pattern: A full round-robin result where every pairing has a strict winner is a total order in disguise -- so the champion is just whichever competitor's row records a win over literally everyone else.
hints: Every pair of teams has a definite stronger/weaker relationship recorded somewhere in the grid, and the champion is the one team nobody beats.
Count how many teams each row beats; because the relation is a total order, exactly one team beats all n - 1 others.
Scan each row and sum it (or count the ones); return the index whose row-sum equals n - 1.

edge_inputs.description: three teams, champion is the middle index
champion sits at the last index, not the first
champion sits at the first index amid a scrambled row order
five teams in a strict chain of dominance

stress.description: a random total ranking is drawn, then the grid is filled consistently with it
